strengths: You effectively used social collaboration skillsâpeer feedback on your design space led to targeted visual improvements.
Your application of the OPVL method shows strong information-literacy skills, with detailed evaluation of each source.
The self-management evidence timeline clearly documents the completion of key milestones, demonstrating organized progress toward your goals.
weaknesses: Your reflection on time management remains descriptive; including specific examples of deadlines met or schedule adjustments would illustrate the impact of your strategies.
Although thorough, your OPVL table does not explicitly link each source evaluation back to your coding learning goal, missing an opportunity to highlight relevance.
Relying on feedback from a small peer group limits perspective diversity; seeking input from teachers or other demographics would broaden your design insights.
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: You show **solid ATL skill application**, particularly in collaboration, information literacy and self-management. To strengthen this section, make your time-management reflections more concrete, explicitly connect source evaluations to your coding objectives and expand your feedback network to ensure diverse viewpoints inform your product development.

strengths: Your learning goal seamlessly integrates coding skills with heart-health knowledge, demonstrating a clear dual focus on technical and subject-matter expertise.
The product goal is well defined: you articulate your target audience, content scope and key user-experience priorities for the website.
You provide a compelling personal contextâlinking the project to your familyâs heart-health experiencesâwhich strongly justifies the relevance and motivation for your work.
Your plan description includes a detailed timeline rationale, showing how sequencing will support organized progress toward the final product.
weaknesses: The success criteria lack measurable targets and timelines (for example, specific site-visit numbers or survey score thresholds) to make evaluation actionable.
The knowledge-review section, while informative, is not concise and isnât explicitly tied back to the success criteria for your website.
The impact-rubric and codes-criteria tables are underdeveloped: they need baseline and target values and clarified levels of code complexity and variety to guide your development.
Your timeline entries, though detailed, omit resource assignments and estimated durationsâadding columns for tools, personnel and time estimates would improve feasibility.
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: Your planning demonstrates **strong alignment between your learning objectives and personal motivation**, combined with a coherent vision for your heart-health website. The detailed timeline rationale further reinforces your organizational approach. To elevate this work, you need to transform broad success statements into **measurable, time-bound criteria**, tighten the connection between your knowledge review and those criteria, and flesh out the rubrics and timeline with clear values and resource allocations.

strengths: You use survey results as concrete evidence to evaluate your productâs impact against your criteria.
Your reflections on developing as an inquirer and applying design principles demonstrate thoughtful self-awareness and learning growth.
You effectively connect your project experience to future DP studies, showing foresight about how these skills will transfer beyond this task.
weaknesses: Your final reflection summarizes positives and negatives but doesnât explicitly compare outcomes to your initial success criteria, weakening the justification for your self-assigned grade.
Self-assigned grades for code complexity lack clear reference to the levels defined in your success criteria table.
Interpretation of your survey data remains superficial; discussing what specific scores mean relative to your defined success levels would deepen the analysis.
Some sections repeat general comments about student developmentâfocus instead on distinct, concrete examples of how particular activities enhanced specific skills.
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: Your reflections demonstrate **authentic engagement with your growth and future applications**, supported by survey evidence. To improve, ensure that your evaluation is tightly benchmarked against the success criteria you set at the startâlink grades and data interpretations directly to those metricsâand replace broad statements with unique, evidence-based examples of your learning trajectory.

annotations.comment: Survey results provide concrete evidence for evaluating impact against your criteria, strengthening your product evaluation.
Final reflection summarizes positives and negatives but could more explicitly compare outcomes to your initial success criteria to justify your selfâgrade.
Your learning goal clearly integrates coding skills with heart health knowledge, showing a focused objective to develop both technical and subjectâmatter expertise.
Your product goal is well defined, detailing audience, content scope, and user experience priorities for the heart health website.
You provide a compelling personal context linked to your familyâs heart health experience, which strongly justifies your projectâs relevance.
The knowledge review is informative but could be more concise and explicitly tied to your success criteria for the website.
The interest and learning goals table repeats narrative content. Integrate this information into a cohesive paragraph to improve clarity.
Your plan description explains why you chose a detailed timeline and how it will support organized progress toward your product.
Your prior skills section highlights design experience but should more clearly connect these skills to your planned HTML development process.
Peer feedback on design space led you to make targeted visual improvements, demonstrating effective use of social collaboration skills.
Success criteria focus on broad impact but lack measurable targets. Consider adding specific metrics (e.g. site visits, survey scores) and timelines.
Your impact rubric is clear but needs baseline and target values for awareness measures to make success criteria actionable.
The codes criteria table is truncated. Clarify levels of code complexity and variety needed at each band to guide your development.
Your reflection on time management is descriptive; include specific examples of deadlines met or adjustments made to illustrate impact.
Your reflection on developing as an inquirer and applying design principles shows thoughtful selfâawareness and learning growth.
Timeline entries are detailed but lack resource assignments and estimated durations. Add columns for tools, personnel, and time estimates.
You applied the OPVL method to evaluate sources rigorously, demonstrating strong informationâliteracy skills with detailed evidence.
The OPVL table is thorough but could explicitly link each source evaluation to your coding learning goal to strengthen relevance.
Your selfâmanagement evidence timeline shows clear progress and completion of key milestones, reinforcing effective planning skills.
Relying on feedback from few peers may limit perspective diversity. Seek input from teachers or other demographics to broaden design insights.
This section repeats ideas about student development. Focus on distinct examples of how specific activities enhanced your skills.
Your selfâassigned grade for code complexity lacks direct reference to your earlier success criteria table; link these clearly.
You connect your project experience to future DP studies, showing foresight and understanding of how these skills transfer beyond this project.
Interpretation of the survey data could be deeper; discuss what a score of â6â means relative to your defined success levels.
Visuals evaluation is honest but doesnât benchmark against your 7â8 criteria; define what âengaged audienceâ metrics you aim to meet.
